title: Aggregation, Grouping, HAVING, and Honest Metrics · Query Trace: Aggregate Functions
summary: A service lead needs ticket counts and response effort by team without double counting or hiding teams with no work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql aggregate functions increment in an audited service-metrics query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.
Create deterministic multi-key ordering and evaluate offset and keyset pagination tradeoffs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 5 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure. A defensible solution applies Aggregate Functions, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an audited service-metrics query pack.

content.code:
WITH effort AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, SUM(minutes_spent) AS minutes
  FROM ticket_events
  GROUP BY ticket_id
)
SELECT teams.name, COUNT(tickets.ticket_id) AS tickets,
       COALESCE(SUM(effort.minutes), 0) AS minutes
FROM teams
LEFT JOIN tickets ON tickets.team_id = teams.team_id
LEFT JOIN effort ON effort.ticket_id = tickets.ticket_id
GROUP BY teams.team_id, teams.name
ORDER BY teams.name;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Aggregate Functions evidence · Theory Aggregate Functions
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-aggregate-functions-up78za" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; aggregate grain, denominator, or reconciliation evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Aggregate Functions into observable performance evidence. For Build Aggregate Functions evidence · Theory Aggregate Functions, keep Aggregate Functions connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-aggregate-functions-up78za
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS unresolved_count
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
GROUP BY team_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
